description_long: This helicopter features a streamlined, angular fuselage with a dark body and prominent blue-tinted cockpit windows wrapping around the front and sides. The main rotor consists of two long, straight blades mounted on a central hub, while the tail boom extends rearward, ending in a hexagonal structure that likely houses the tail rotor. The landing skids are simple, straight bars with cross supports, providing stability for ground contact. The cockpit windows are large and sharply angled, offering wide visibility for the pilot and co-pilot. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal surface detailing and a focus on geometric simplicity. No visible registration numbers or markings are present on the exterior.
description: A dark, angular helicopter with blue-tinted cockpit windows, two main rotor blades, and simple landing skids. The design is geometric and minimal, with a hexagonal tail structure.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide, blue-tinted cockpit windows and the symmetrical landing skids beneath the fuselage.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the elongated body, two main rotor blades, and the hexagonal tail structure, with the cockpit windows clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the cockpit windows, the main rotor hub, and the parallel landing skids.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the cockpit windows, main rotor, and tail structure, with landing skids visible below.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, straight main rotor blades, likely made of composite or metal, colored black with no visible markings. The blades are mounted on a central hub and extend well beyond the width of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_rotor: Tail rotor is not clearly visible, but the hexagonal structure at the end of the tail boom suggests an enclosed or fenestron-type tail rotor configuration.
attribute_landing_skids: Landing skids are straight, black bars with cross supports, made of metal or composite material, and provide a stable base for landing.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Four large, blue-tinted cockpit windows wrap around the front and sides, sharply angled with black framing, offering wide visibility and no visible markings.
